"Christ is the Rock (Deut. 32:3-4, 18, 30-31; 1 Cor. 10:1-4), or the Rock of Heaven. (Moses 7:53.) Such name-titles carry a connotation of strength and stability (1 Sam. 2:2), as for instance when David exulted: 'The Lord is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer; The God of my rock, in him will I trust: he is my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my high tower, and my refuge, my saviour.'…

To the chief Musician, A Psalm. of David, the servant of the Lord, who spake unto the Lord the words of. this. song in the day that the Lord delivered him from the hand of. all. his enemies, and from the hand of Saul; And he said,
40 (34) The Lord is worthy to be praised for His judgements with the mouth of His pious ones; And blessed be Israel of the Lord for ever.

Such imagery is figurative. The same old Jewish imagery, or figure of speech, is used and its meaning made clear when it was said of the Lord, "And he rode upon a cherub, and did fly: and he was seen upon the wings of the wind" (2 Samuel 22:11). The same metaphorical use of "wings of the wind" is found in Psalms (see Psalm 18:10). As the wind has wings, so do cherubs have figurative wings. (Oscar…

I cannot save you. I can tell you how to save yourselves, but you must
do the will of God. 10:317.

R. Berechiah began the discussion in R. Levi’s name with the verse: Thou hast also given me Thy shield, and Thy salvation; and Thy right hand hath holden me up, and Thy condescension hath made me great (Ps. 18:36). The Holy One, blessed be He, stood as He revealed Himself to Abraham, while Abraham remained seated, as it is said: And he sat (Gen. 18:1). The Holy One, blessed be He, declared: Do…

call a nation that thou knowest not. The covenant people will take the gospel to the Gentiles, who will help take the word throughout the world. Compare this phrase to Ps. 18:43: "Thou has made me the head of the heathen: a people whom I have not known shall serve me." (Donald W. Parry, Jay A. Parry, and Tina M. Peterson, Understanding Isaiah, p.491)
